content: Devon flagged Solana mainnet RPC returning 429s at 14:32 UTC. Memory commit pipeline backing up. Ari already investigating with Helius as contingency.
summary: Solana RPC rate limit incident detected

content: Solana public RPC endpoints throttle at ~300 req/s. Our chain commit pipeline hits that under load. Helius dedicated nodes remove that bottleneck entirely.
summary: Root cause: public RPC throughput ceiling vs pipeline demand

content: When RPC endpoints 429: check current provider's rate limits, confirm pipeline queue depth in DataDog, then evaluate dedicated node cost vs SLA impact. Document the decision.
summary: RPC incident response process: monitor → verify → cost-benefit → decide

content: Ari presented Helius migration path to me and Devon at 10am on the 15th. Dedicated node cost: $200/mo. Cutover feasible same day via config swap in Linear.
summary: Helius migration scope discussed with Ari

content: Helius RPC pricing: $200/mo for dedicated Solana node includes guaranteed throughput, no rate limiting, and faster failover. Added to AWS monthly bill.
summary: Helius cost model and SLA guarantees documented

content: RPC provider migrations: swap endpoint URLs in config → deploy to prod → monitor commit latency for 30min → verify no queue backlog in metrics before marking resolved.
summary: Standard RPC failover checklist for future use

content: Helius cutover at 16:45 UTC on the 16th. Commit queue drained within 8min. No customer impact reported. Slack thread marked closed by Ari.
Public RPC limits expose us to traffic spikes. Dedicated nodes are table stakes for production Solana pipelines. Budget this as operational overhead, not occasional expense.
summary: Lesson: dedicate RPC provider for blockchain infrastructure

content: Document all vendor migrations and cost deltas in Notion ops wiki under 'Infrastructure Changes'. Include date, provider, reason, monthly cost. Anya reconciles against invoice.
I keep pushing for async RCAs rather than live postmortems. Ari and Devon prefer written summaries anyway. Less repeat explanations, better for async team.
Memory commit pipeline is critical path. Any RPC latency or rate limit cascades into customer-visible delays. Monitor commit queue depth continuously in DataDog.
summary: Memory commits are critical dependency, require dedicated RPC

content: I hate re-explaining why we chose Helius over cheaper alternatives. $200/mo is cheap insurance against another incident. Document it once, reference forever.
summary: Cost decisions require once-and-done justification docs
